title: Mahonia longipes
taxonomy: Mahonia longipes is classified in the family Berberidaceae. This plant is a species of Mahonia, a genus of flowering plants native to western North America. The Berberidaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as barberry or Oregon grape family. Mahonia longipes is a type of shrub that produces yellow flowers and red berries. Its classification is further detailed in the kingdom Plantae and the order Ranunculales.

intro: Mahonia longipes, also known as Oregon grape, is a flowering evergreen shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Mahonia longipes contains a range of bioactive constituents, including berberine, a compound with known antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as alkaloids, phenolic acids, and glycosides. The most common herbal preparations made with Mahonia longipes include extracts, capsules, and tinctures, often used to support digestive health and immune function.
